revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:放射能泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11498934]] Wikidata name: radioactive hot spring [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This is questionable as I think it requires [[WP:MEDRS]] and I am surprised that jawiki has these claims not only not medically sourced but without medically reliable sources[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:10, 14 March 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:Misasa onsen02n4592.jpg|thumb|right|Kawaraburo at {{ill|Misasa Onsen|ja|三朝温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misasa Onsen|qq=}} , known for its radon spring]] '''Radioactive Spring''' (放射能泉, Hōshanōsen) is a classification of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] based on the {{ill|Hot Spring Types|ja|温泉の泉質|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Types|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} labeling system. According to the {{ill|Onsen Act|ja|温泉法|1=Onsen Act|1=Onsen Act|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Act|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Act|qq=}}, it is categorized as a {{ill|medical spring|ja|温泉#療養泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/medical spring|qq=|1=onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q655311}} due to the presence of special components.<ref name="JBSJ" /> Approximately 8% of hot springs in Japan fall under this category.<ref name="JBSJ" />
Radioactive spring are often collectively referred to as "radium springs" (ラジウム泉), regardless of their radium content. However, in Japan, most such springs are rich in radon and are commonly called {{ill|Radon Hot Springs|ja|ラドン温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radon Hot Springs|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ラドン|1=radon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1133}} (ラドン泉). Springs containing high levels of radon's isotope, {{ill|Thoron|ja|ラドン220|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thoron|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ラドンの同位体|1=isotope of radon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2334543}}, are also referred to as "Thoron Hot Springs" (トロン泉).<ref>[https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ue3t-cb/bbs/special/sience_of_hotspring/sience_of_hotspring_4-6.htm The Science of Hot Springs: About Radioactive Springs] (関東周辺立ち寄り温泉みしゅらん)</ref> == Definition == {| class="wikitable floatright" style="margin:1em; font-size:95%" |+ Comparison of Old and New Labels<ref>{{Cite web|title=Reference Material: Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=Natural Environment Bureau, Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2018-03-26 }} </ref> ! scope="col" | Old Label ! scope="col" | New Label ! scope="col" | Abbreviated Label |- style="background-color:#eef" | 放射能泉 | {{Plainlist| * Simple Weak Radioactive Spring * Simple Radioactive Spring * Weak Radioactive Spring with Components (e.g., Na, Cl) * Radioactive Spring with Components (e.g., Na, Cl) }}
| {{Plainlist| * Simple Weak Rn Spring * Simple Rn Spring * Weak Rn-Component Spring * Rn-Component Spring }}
|}
; Radioactive Spring : Contains at least 3 nanocuries of radon per kilogram of water (equivalent to 8.25 {{ill|Mache units|en|Mache unit|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mache units|qq=}} or 111 {{ill|Becquerel|en|Bq|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Becquerel|qq=}}).<ref name="JBSJ">{{Cite journal|title=Radioactivity and Hot Springs: Onsen Science |author=Masayoshi Yamamoto, Junpei Tomita |journal=Onsen Science |publisher=Japan Society of Hot Spring Science |volume=64 |issue=4 |pages=388-401 |naid=40020464241 |year=2015 }} </ref>
; Weak Radioactive Spring : Contains 8.25–50 Mache units of radon per kilogram. ; Radioactive Spring : Contains 50 Mache units or more of radon per kilogram.
If these criteria are met, the onsen is categorized as a radioactive spring, and many springs are labeled as “含放射能○○泉” or “含弱放射能□□泉” (e.g., “Weak Rn-Na-Cl Spring”). The highest recorded level in Japan is at the Tenmangu Spring in [[:en:Arima Onsen|Arima Onsen]] , with 14 Bq/L.<ref name=JBSJ />
== Usage == {{main|:en:Radiation hormesis#Environmental Use of Radioactive Springs}}
Radioactive spring contain trace amounts of {{ill|radium|en|Radium|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/radium|qq=}} , {{ill|radon|en|Radon|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/radon|qq=}} , {{ill|astatine|en|Astatine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astatine|qq=}} , and other radioactive isotopes from {{ill|nuclear decay|en|Decay (radioactivity)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nuclear decay|qq=}} . For instance, near the source at Arima Onsen, exposure levels reach 13 μSv/h, which is comparable to levels requiring indoor sheltering under Japan's nuclear safety guidelines.
Proponents claim no adverse health effects and argue for {{ill|hormesis|en|Radiation hormesis|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hormesis|qq=}} effects, asserting that radiation activates immune cells and promotes health. Critics point out potential health risks from radon exposure. Thus, radioactive springs and the hormesis theory remain subjects of both support and skepticism.
One study estimated a radiation dose of 2.8 nSv from a 2-hour bath in water containing 3.9 Bq/kg of radon. Adding 500 ml of drinking water from the spring increases the dose to 5.1 nSv. At {{ill|Masutomi Onsen|ja|増富温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masutomi Onsen|qq=}} , drinking 2.2 L/day of water with 617 mBq/L radon results in an annual dose of 0.14 mSv.<ref name=JBSJ />
== Health Benefits == ''Note: [[Health effects of hot springs|Effects]] are not guaranteed for all individuals.''
=== Indications === ==== Bathing ==== * {{ill|General Indications for hot spring use|lt=General indications|ja|一般的適応症|1=General Indications for hot spring use|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|qq=}}, {{ill|Gout|en|Gout|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gout|qq=}} , {{ill|Hypertension|en|High blood pressure|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hypertension|qq=}} , {{ill|Atherosclerosis|en|Atherosclerosis|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atherosclerosis|qq=}} , chronic {{ill|Skin disease|en|Skin disease|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Skin disease|qq=}} , chronic {{ill|Gynecological diseases|en|Gynecology|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gynecological diseases|qq=}} , chronic {{ill|Cholecystitis|en|Cholecystitis|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cholecystitis|qq=}} , and {{ill|Cholelithiasis|en|Gallstone|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cholelithiasis|qq=}} . ==== Drinking ==== * Gout, chronic gastrointestinal diseases, chronic cholecystitis, gallstones, {{ill|Neuralgia|en|Neuralgia|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Neuralgia|qq=}}, {{ill|Muscle pain|en|Myalgia|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muscle pain|qq=}} , and joint pain. === Contraindications === * General contraindications for {{ill|Hot springs|ja|温泉#一般的禁忌症|1=General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|lt=General Contraindications for hot spring use|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot springs|13=User:Immanuelle/General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|qq=}} apply. == Notable Hot Springs == === In Japan === * Futamata Radium Onsen (Hokkaido) * {{ill|Misasa Onsen|ja|三朝温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misasa Onsen|qq=}}(Tottori)
* {{ill|Sekigane Onsen|ja|関金温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekigane Onsen|qq=}}(Tottori)
* [[:en:Arima Onsen|Arima Onsen]](Hyōgo)
* {{ill|Masutomi Onsen|ja|増富温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masutomi Onsen|qq=}}(Yamanashi)
* [[:en:Tamagawa Hot Spring|Tamagawa Onsen (Akita)]](Akita)
* Ena Gorge Onsen (Gifu) * Ikeda Radium Onsen (Shimane) * {{ill|Bobata Onsen|ja|母畑温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bobata Onsen|qq=}}(Fukushima)
* {{ill|Nekodaki Onsen|ja|猫啼温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekodaki Onsen|qq=}}(Fukushima)
* Baba-no-Yu Onsen (Fukushima)
=== Outside Japan === * {{ill|Arkaroola|en|Arkaroola|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arkaroola|qq=}}– Australia
* [[:en:Ramsar,_Iran|Ramsar, Iran]]– Known for its radium-rich springs and high natural radiation.<ref name="ra">{{Cite journal|title=Exposure to 226Ra from Vegetable Consumption in Ramsar |url=https://doi.org/10.1016/S0265-931X(02)00108-X }} </ref>
* {{ill|Radium Hot Springs|en|Radium Hot Springs|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radium Hot Springs|qq=}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Radiation hormesis|en|Radiation hormesis|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radiation hormesis|qq=}}
* {{ill|Natural radiation|en|Natural radiation|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natural radiation|qq=}}
* {{ill|Environmental radiation|en|Environmental radiation|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Environmental radiation|qq=}}
* {{ill|Natural radioactivity|en|Natural radioactivity|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natural radioactivity|qq=}}
* [[:en:Radium_Springs|Radium Springs]]
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Onsen}}

|}
; Radioactive Spring : Contains at least 3 nanocuries of radon per kilogram of water (equivalent to 8.25 {{ill|Mache units|en|Mache unit|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mache units|qq=}} or 111 {{ill|Becquerel|en|Bq|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Becquerel|qq=}}).<ref name="JBSJ">{{Cite journal|title=Radioactivity and Hot Springs: Onsen Science |author=Masayoshi Yamamoto, Junpei Tomita |journal=Onsen Science |publisher=Japan Society of Hot Spring Science |volume=64 |issue=4 |pages=388-401 |naid=40020464241 |year=2015 }} </ref>
; Weak Radioactive Spring : Contains 8.25–50 Mache units of radon per kilogram. ; Radioactive Spring : Contains 50 Mache units or more of radon per kilogram.
If these criteria are met, the onsen is categorized as a radioactive spring, and many springs are labeled as “含放射能○○泉” or “含弱放射能□□泉” (e.g., “Weak Rn-Na-Cl Spring”). The highest recorded level in Japan is at the Tenmangu Spring in [[:en:Arima Onsen|Arima Onsen]] , with 14 Bq/L.<ref name=JBSJ />

Proponents claim no adverse health effects and argue for {{ill|hormesis|en|Radiation hormesis|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hormesis|qq=}} effects, asserting that radiation activates immune cells and promotes health. Critics point out potential health risks from radon exposure. Thus, radioactive springs and the hormesis theory remain subjects of both support and skepticism.
One study estimated a radiation dose of 2.8 nSv from a 2-hour bath in water containing 3.9 Bq/kg of radon. Adding 500 ml of drinking water from the spring increases the dose to 5.1 nSv. At {{ill|Masutomi Onsen|ja|増富温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masutomi Onsen|qq=}} , drinking 2.2 L/day of water with 617 mBq/L radon results in an annual dose of 0.14 mSv.<ref name=JBSJ />
== Health Benefits == ''Note: [[Health effects of hot springs|Effects]] are not guaranteed for all individuals.''
=== Indications === ==== Bathing ==== * {{ill|General Indications for hot spring use|lt=General indications|ja|一般的適応症|1=General Indications for hot spring use|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|qq=}}, {{ill|Gout|en|Gout|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gout|qq=}} , {{ill|Hypertension|en|High blood pressure|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hypertension|qq=}} , {{ill|Atherosclerosis|en|Atherosclerosis|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atherosclerosis|qq=}} , chronic {{ill|Skin disease|en|Skin disease|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Skin disease|qq=}} , chronic {{ill|Gynecological diseases|en|Gynecology|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gynecological diseases|qq=}} , chronic {{ill|Cholecystitis|en|Cholecystitis|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cholecystitis|qq=}} , and {{ill|Cholelithiasis|en|Gallstone|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cholelithiasis|qq=}} . ==== Drinking ==== * Gout, chronic gastrointestinal diseases, chronic cholecystitis, gallstones, {{ill|Neuralgia|en|Neuralgia|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Neuralgia|qq=}}, {{ill|Muscle pain|en|Myalgia|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muscle pain|qq=}} , and joint pain. === Contraindications === * General contraindications for {{ill|Hot springs|ja|温泉#一般的禁忌症|1=General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|lt=General Contraindications for hot spring use|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot springs|13=User:Immanuelle/General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|qq=}} apply. == Notable Hot Springs == === In Japan === * Futamata Radium Onsen (Hokkaido) * {{ill|Misasa <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:放射能泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11498934]] Wikidata name: radioactive hot spring [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This is questionable as I think it requires [[WP:MEDRS]] and I am surprised that jawiki has these claims not only not medically sourced but without medically reliable sources[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:10, 14 March 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:Misasa onsen02n4592.jpg|thumb|right|Kawaraburo at {{ill|Misasa Onsen|ja|三朝温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misasa Onsen|qq=}} , known for its radon spring]] '''Radioactive Spring''' (放射能泉, Hōshanōsen) is a classification of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] based on the {{ill|Hot Spring Types|ja|温泉の泉質|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Types|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} labeling system. According to the {{ill|Onsen Act|ja|温泉法|1=Onsen Act|1=Onsen Act|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Act|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Act|qq=}}, it is categorized as a {{ill|medical spring|ja|温泉#療養泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/medical spring|1=onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q655311}} due to the presence of special components.<ref name="JBSJ" /> Approximately 8% of hot springs in Japan fall under this category.<ref name="JBSJ" />
Radioactive spring are often collectively referred to as "radium springs" (ラジウム泉), regardless of their radium content. However, in Japan, most such springs are rich in radon and are commonly called {{ill|Radon Hot Springs|ja|ラドン温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radon Hot Springs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ラドン|1=radon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1133}} (ラドン泉). Springs containing high levels of radon's isotope, {{ill|Thoron|ja|ラドン220|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thoron|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ラドンの同位体|1=isotope of radon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2334543}}, are also referred to as "Thoron Hot Springs" (トロン泉).<ref>[https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ue3t-cb/bbs/special/sience_of_hotspring/sience_of_hotspring_4-6.htm The Science of Hot Springs: About Radioactive Springs] (関東周辺立ち寄り温泉みしゅらん)</ref> == Definition == {| class="wikitable floatright" style="margin:1em; font-size:95%" |+ Comparison of Old and New Labels<ref>{{Cite web|title=Reference Material: Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=Natural Environment Bureau, Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2018-03-26 }} </ref> ! scope="col" | Old Label ! scope="col" | New Label ! scope="col" | Abbreviated Label |- style="background-color:#eef" | 放射能泉 | {{Plainlist| * Simple Weak Radioactive Spring * Simple Radioactive Spring * Weak Radioactive Spring with Components (e.g., Na, Cl) * Radioactive Spring with Components (e.g., Na, Cl) }}
| {{Plainlist| * Simple Weak Rn Spring * Simple Rn Spring * Weak Rn-Component Spring * Rn-Component Spring }}
|}
; Radioactive Spring : Contains at least 3 nanocuries of radon per kilogram of water (equivalent to 8.25 {{ill|Mache units|en|Mache unit|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mache units|qq=}} or 111 {{ill|Becquerel|en|Bq|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Becquerel|qq=}}).<ref name="JBSJ">{{Cite journal|title=Radioactivity and Hot Springs: Onsen Science |author=Masayoshi Yamamoto, Junpei Tomita |journal=Onsen Science |publisher=Japan Society of Hot Spring Science |volume=64 |issue=4 |pages=388-401 |naid=40020464241 |year=2015 }} </ref>
; Weak Radioactive Spring : Contains 8.25–50 Mache units of radon per kilogram. ; Radioactive Spring : Contains 50 Mache units or more of radon per kilogram.
If these criteria are met, the onsen is categorized as a radioactive spring, and many springs are labeled as “含放射能○○泉” or “含弱放射能□□泉” (e.g., “Weak Rn-Na-Cl Spring”). The highest recorded level in Japan is at the Tenmangu Spring in [[:en:Arima Onsen|Arima Onsen]] , with 14 Bq/L.<ref name=JBSJ />
== Usage == {{main|:en:Radiation hormesis#Environmental Use of Radioactive Springs}}
Radioactive spring contain trace amounts of {{ill|radium|en|Radium|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/radium|qq=}} , {{ill|radon|en|Radon|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/radon|qq=}} , {{ill|astatine|en|Astatine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astatine|qq=}} , and other radioactive isotopes from {{ill|nuclear decay|en|Decay (radioactivity)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nuclear decay|qq=}} . For instance, near the source at Arima Onsen, exposure levels reach 13 μSv/h, which is comparable to levels requiring indoor sheltering under Japan's nuclear safety guidelines.
Proponents claim no adverse health effects and argue for {{ill|hormesis|en|Radiation hormesis|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hormesis|qq=}} effects, asserting that radiation activates immune cells and promotes health. Critics point out potential health risks from radon exposure. Thus, radioactive springs and the hormesis theory remain subjects of both support and skepticism.
One study estimated a radiation dose of 2.8 nSv from a 2-hour bath in water containing 3.9 Bq/kg of radon. Adding 500 ml of drinking water from the spring increases the dose to 5.1 nSv. At {{ill|Masutomi Onsen|ja|増富温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masutomi Onsen|qq=}} , drinking 2.2 L/day of water with 617 mBq/L radon results in an annual dose of 0.14 mSv.<ref name=JBSJ />
== Health Benefits == ''Note: [[Health effects of hot springs|Effects]] are not guaranteed for all individuals.''

* {{ill|Sekigane Onsen|ja|関金温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekigane Onsen|qq=}}(Tottori)

* [[:en:Tamagawa Hot Spring|Tamagawa Onsen (Akita)]](Akita)
* Ena Gorge Onsen (Gifu) * Ikeda Radium Onsen (Shimane) * {{ill|Bobata Onsen|ja|母畑温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bobata Onsen|qq=}}(Fukushima)
* {{ill|Nekodaki Onsen|ja|猫啼温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekodaki Onsen|qq=}}(Fukushima)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:放射能泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11498934]] Wikidata name: radioactive hot spring [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This is questionable as I think it requires [[WP:MEDRS]] and I am surprised that jawiki has these claims not only not medically sourced but without medically reliable sources[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:10, 14 March 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:Misasa onsen02n4592.jpg|thumb|right|Kawaraburo at {{ill|Misasa Onsen|ja|三朝温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misasa Onsen|qq=}} , known for its radon spring]] '''Radioactive Spring''' (放射能泉, Hōshanōsen) is a classification of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] based on the {{ill|Hot Spring Types|ja|温泉の泉質|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Types|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} labeling system. According to the {{ill|Onsen Act|ja|温泉法|1=Onsen Act|1=Onsen Act|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Act|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Act|qq=}}, it is categorized as a {{ill|medical spring|ja|温泉#療養泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/medical spring|1=onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q655311}} due to the presence of special components.<ref name="JBSJ" /> Approximately 8% of hot springs in Japan fall under this category.<ref name="JBSJ" />
Radioactive spring are often collectively referred to as "radium springs" (ラジウム泉), regardless of their radium content. However, in Japan, most such springs are rich in radon and are commonly called {{ill|Radon Hot Springs|ja|ラドン温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radon Hot Springs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ラドン|1=radon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1133}} (ラドン泉). Springs containing high levels of radon's isotope, {{ill|Thoron|ja|ラドン220|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thoron|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ラドンの同位体|1=isotope of radon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2334543}}, are also referred to as "Thoron Hot Springs" (トロン泉).<ref>[https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ue3t-cb/bbs/special/sience_of_hotspring/sience_of_hotspring_4-6.htm The Science of Hot Springs: About Radioactive Springs] (関東周辺立ち寄り温泉みしゅらん)</ref> == Definition == {| class="wikitable floatright" style="margin:1em; font-size:95%" |+ Comparison of Old and New Labels<ref>{{Cite web|title=Reference Material: Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=Natural Environment Bureau, Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2018-03-26 }} </ref> ! scope="col" | Old Label ! scope="col" | New Label ! scope="col" | Abbreviated Label |- style="background-color:#eef" | 放射能泉 | {{Plainlist| * Simple Weak Radioactive Spring * Simple Radioactive Spring * Weak Radioactive Spring with Components (e.g., Na, Cl) * Radioactive Spring with Components (e.g., Na, Cl) }}
| {{Plainlist| * Simple Weak Rn Spring * Simple Rn Spring * Weak Rn-Component Spring * Rn-Component Spring }}
|}
; Radioactive Spring : Contains at least 3 nanocuries of radon per kilogram of water (equivalent to 8.25 {{ill|Mache units|en|Mache unit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mache units|qq=}} or 111 {{ill|Becquerel|en|Bq|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Becquerel|qq=}}).<ref name="JBSJ">{{Cite journal|title=Radioactivity and Hot Springs: Onsen Science |author=Masayoshi Yamamoto, Junpei Tomita |journal=Onsen Science |publisher=Japan Society of Hot Spring Science |volume=64 |issue=4 |pages=388-401 |naid=40020464241 |year=2015 }} </ref>
; Weak Radioactive Spring : Contains 8.25–50 Mache units of radon per kilogram. ; Radioactive Spring : Contains 50 Mache units or more of radon per kilogram.
If these criteria are met, the onsen is categorized as a radioactive spring, and many springs are labeled as “含放射能○○泉” or “含弱放射能□□泉” (e.g., “Weak Rn-Na-Cl Spring”). The highest recorded level in Japan is at the Tenmangu Spring in [[:en:Arima Onsen|Arima Onsen]] , with 14 Bq/L.<ref name=JBSJ />
== Usage == {{main|:en:Radiation hormesis#Environmental Use of Radioactive Springs}}
Radioactive spring contain trace amounts of {{ill|radium|en|Radium|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/radium|qq=}} , {{ill|radon|en|Radon|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/radon|qq=}} , {{ill|astatine|en|Astatine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astatine|qq=}} , and other radioactive isotopes from {{ill|nuclear decay|en|Decay (radioactivity)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nuclear decay|qq=}} . For instance, near the source at Arima Onsen, exposure levels reach 13 μSv/h, which is comparable to levels requiring indoor sheltering under Japan's nuclear safety guidelines.
Proponents claim no adverse health effects and argue for {{ill|hormesis|en|Radiation hormesis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hormesis|qq=}} effects, asserting that radiation activates immune cells and promotes health. Critics point out potential health risks from radon exposure. Thus, radioactive springs and the hormesis theory remain subjects of both support and skepticism.
One study estimated a radiation dose of 2.8 nSv from a 2-hour bath in water containing 3.9 Bq/kg of radon. Adding 500 ml of drinking water from the spring increases the dose to 5.1 nSv. At {{ill|Masutomi Onsen|ja|増富温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masutomi Onsen|qq=}} , drinking 2.2 L/day of water with 617 mBq/L radon results in an annual dose of 0.14 mSv.<ref name=JBSJ />
== Health Benefits == ''Note: [[Health effects of hot springs|Effects]] are not guaranteed for all individuals.''

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:放射能泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11498934]] Wikidata name: radioactive hot spring [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This is questionable as I think it requires [[WP:MEDRS]] and I am surprised that jawiki has these claims not only not medically sourced but without medically reliable sources[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:10, 14 March 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:Misasa onsen02n4592.jpg|thumb|right|Kawaraburo at {{ill|Misasa Onsen|ja|三朝温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misasa Onsen|qq=}} , known for its radon spring]] '''Radioactive Spring''' (放射能泉, Hōshanōsen) is a classification of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] based on the {{ill|Hot Spring Types|ja|温泉の泉質|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Types|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} labeling system. According to the {{ill|Onsen Act|ja|温泉法|1=Onsen Act|1=Onsen Act|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Act|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Act|qq=}}, it is categorized as a {{ill|medical spring|ja|温泉#療養泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/medical spring|1=onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q655311}} due to the presence of special components.<ref name="JBSJ" /> Approximately 8% of hot springs in Japan fall under this category.<ref name="JBSJ" />
Radioactive spring are often collectively referred to as "radium springs" (ラジウム泉), regardless of their radium content. However, in Japan, most such springs are rich in radon and are commonly called {{ill|Radon Hot Springs|ja|ラドン温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radon Hot Springs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ラドン|1=radon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1133}} (ラドン泉). Springs containing high levels of radon's isotope, [[:en:Isotopes_of_radon|Thoron]], are also referred to as "Thoron Hot Springs" (トロン泉).<ref>[https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ue3t-cb/bbs/special/sience_of_hotspring/sience_of_hotspring_4-6.htm The Science of Hot Springs: About Radioactive Springs] (関東周辺立ち寄り温泉みしゅらん)</ref> == Definition == {| class="wikitable floatright" style="margin:1em; font-size:95%" |+ Comparison of Old and New Labels<ref>{{Cite web|title=Reference Material: Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=Natural Environment Bureau, Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2018-03-26 }} </ref> ! scope="col" | Old Label ! scope="col" | New Label ! scope="col" | Abbreviated Label |- style="background-color:#eef" | 放射能泉 | {{Plainlist| * Simple Weak Radioactive Spring * Simple Radioactive Spring * Weak Radioactive Spring with Components (e.g., Na, Cl) * Radioactive Spring with Components (e.g., Na, Cl) }}
| {{Plainlist| * Simple Weak Rn Spring * Simple Rn Spring * Weak Rn-Component Spring * Rn-Component Spring }}
|}
; Radioactive Spring : Contains at least 3 nanocuries of radon per kilogram of water (equivalent to 8.25 {{ill|Mache units|en|Mache unit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mache units|qq=}} or 111 {{ill|Becquerel|en|Bq|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Becquerel|qq=}}).<ref name="JBSJ">{{Cite journal|title=Radioactivity and Hot Springs: Onsen Science |author=Masayoshi Yamamoto, Junpei Tomita |journal=Onsen Science |publisher=Japan Society of Hot Spring Science |volume=64 |issue=4 |pages=388-401 |naid=40020464241 |year=2015 }} </ref>
; Weak Radioactive Spring : Contains 8.25–50 Mache units of radon per kilogram. ; Radioactive Spring : Contains 50 Mache units or more of radon per kilogram.
If these criteria are met, the onsen is categorized as a radioactive spring, and many springs are labeled as “含放射能○○泉” or “含弱放射能□□泉” (e.g., “Weak Rn-Na-Cl Spring”). The highest recorded level in Japan is at the Tenmangu Spring in [[:en:Arima Onsen|Arima Onsen]] , with 14 Bq/L.<ref name=JBSJ />
== Usage == {{main|:en:Radiation hormesis#Environmental Use of Radioactive Springs}}
Radioactive spring contain trace amounts of {{ill|radium|en|Radium|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/radium|qq=}} , {{ill|radon|en|Radon|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/radon|qq=}} , {{ill|astatine|en|Astatine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astatine|qq=}} , and other radioactive isotopes from {{ill|nuclear decay|en|Decay (radioactivity)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nuclear decay|qq=}} . For instance, near the source at Arima Onsen, exposure levels reach 13 μSv/h, which is comparable to levels requiring indoor sheltering under Japan's nuclear safety guidelines.
Proponents claim no adverse health effects and argue for {{ill|hormesis|en|Radiation hormesis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hormesis|qq=}} effects, asserting that radiation activates immune cells and promotes health. Critics point out potential health risks from radon exposure. Thus, radioactive springs and the hormesis theory remain subjects of both support and skepticism.
One study estimated a radiation dose of 2.8 nSv from a 2-hour bath in water containing 3.9 Bq/kg of radon. Adding 500 ml of drinking water from the spring increases the dose to 5.1 nSv. At {{ill|Masutomi Onsen|ja|増富温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masutomi Onsen|qq=}} , drinking 2.2 L/day of water with 617 mBq/L radon results in an annual dose of 0.14 mSv.<ref name=JBSJ />
== Health Benefits == ''Note: [[Health effects of hot springs|Effects]] are not guaranteed for all individuals.''
=== Indications === ==== Bathing ==== * {{ill|General Indications for hot spring use|lt=General indications|ja|一般的適応症|1=General Indications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|qq=}}, {{ill|Gout|en|Gout|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gout|qq=}} , {{ill|Hypertension|en|High blood pressure|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hypertension|qq=}} , {{ill|Atherosclerosis|en|Atherosclerosis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atherosclerosis|qq=}} , chronic {{ill|Skin disease|en|Skin disease|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Skin disease|qq=}} , chronic {{ill|Gynecological diseases|en|Gynecology|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gynecological diseases|qq=}} , chronic {{ill|Cholecystitis|en|Cholecystitis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cholecystitis|qq=}} , and {{ill|Cholelithiasis|en|Gallstone|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cholelithiasis|qq=}} . ==== Drinking ==== * Gout, chronic gastrointestinal diseases, chronic cholecystitis, gallstones, {{ill|Neuralgia|en|Neuralgia|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Neuralgia|qq=}}, {{ill|Muscle pain|en|Myalgia|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muscle pain|qq=}} , and joint pain. === Contraindications === * General contraindications for {{ill|Hot springs|ja|温泉#一般的禁忌症|1=General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|lt=General Contraindications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot springs|13=User:Immanuelle/General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|qq=}} apply. == Notable Hot Springs == === In Japan === * Futamata Radium Onsen (Hokkaido) * {{ill|Misasa Onsen|ja|三朝温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misasa Onsen|qq=}}(Tottori)
* {{ill|Sekigane Onsen|ja|関金温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekigane Onsen|qq=}}(Tottori)
* [[:en:Arima Onsen|Arima Onsen]](Hyōgo)
* {{ill|Masutomi Onsen|ja|増富温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masutomi Onsen|qq=}}(Yamanashi)
* [[:en:Tamagawa Hot Spring|Tamagawa Onsen (Akita)]](Akita)
* Ena Gorge Onsen (Gifu) * Ikeda Radium Onsen (Shimane) * {{ill|Bobata Onsen|ja|母畑温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bobata Onsen|qq=}}(Fukushima)
* {{ill|Nekodaki Onsen|ja|猫啼温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekodaki Onsen|qq=}}(Fukushima)
* Baba-no-Yu Onsen (Fukushima)
=== Outside Japan === * {{ill|Arkaroola|en|Arkaroola|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arkaroola|qq=}}– Australia
* [[:en:Ramsar,_Iran|Ramsar, Iran]]– Known for its radium-rich springs and high natural radiation.<ref name="ra">{{Cite journal|title=Exposure to 226Ra from Vegetable Consumption in Ramsar |url=https://doi.org/10.1016/S0265-931X(02)00108-X }} </ref>
* {{ill|Radium Hot Springs|en|Radium Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radium Hot Springs|qq=}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Radiation hormesis|en|Radiation hormesis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radiation hormesis|qq=}}
* {{ill|Natural radiation|en|Natural radiation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natural radiation|qq=}}
* {{ill|Environmental radiation|en|Environmental radiation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Environmental radiation|qq=}}
* {{ill|Natural radioactivity|en|Natural radioactivity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natural radioactivity|qq=}}

=== Indications === ==== Bathing ==== * {{ill|General Indications for hot spring use|lt=General indications|ja|一般的適応症|1=General Indications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|qq=}}, {{ill|Gout|en|Gout|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gout|qq=}} , {{ill|Hypertension|en|High blood pressure|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hypertension|qq=}} , {{ill|Atherosclerosis|en|Atherosclerosis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atherosclerosis|qq=}} , chronic {{ill|Skin disease|en|Skin disease|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Skin disease|qq=}} , chronic {{ill|Gynecological diseases|en|Gynecology|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gynecological diseases|qq=}} , chronic {{ill|Cholecystitis|en|Cholecystitis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cholecystitis|qq=}} , and {{ill|Cholelithiasis|en|Gallstone|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cholelithiasis|qq=}} . ==== Drinking ==== * Gout, chronic gastrointestinal diseases, chronic cholecystitis, gallstones, {{ill|Neuralgia|en|Neuralgia|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Neuralgia|qq=}}, {{ill|Muscle pain|en|Myalgia|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muscle pain|qq=}} , and joint pain. === Contraindications === * General contraindications for {{ill|Hot springs|ja|温泉#一般的禁忌症|1=General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|lt=General Contraindications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot springs|13=User:Immanuelle/General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|qq=}} apply. == Notable Hot Springs == === In Japan === * Futamata Radium Onsen (Hokkaido) * {{ill|Misasa Onsen|ja|三朝温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misasa Onsen|qq=}}(Tottori)
* {{ill|Sekigane Onsen|ja|関金温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekigane Onsen|qq=}}(Tottori)
* [[:en:Arima Onsen|Arima Onsen]](Hyōgo)
* {{ill|Masutomi Onsen|ja|増富温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masutomi Onsen|qq=}}(Yamanashi)
* [[:en:Tamagawa Hot Spring|Tamagawa Onsen (Akita)]](Akita)
* Ena Gorge Onsen (Gifu) * Ikeda Radium Onsen (Shimane) * {{ill|Bobata Onsen|ja|母畑温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bobata Onsen|qq=}}(Fukushima)
* {{ill|Nekodaki Onsen|ja|猫啼温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekodaki Onsen|qq=}}(Fukushima)
* Baba-no-Yu Onsen (Fukushima)
=== Outside Japan === * {{ill|Arkaroola|en|Arkaroola|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arkaroola|qq=}}– Australia
* [[:en:Ramsar,_Iran|Ramsar, Iran]]– Known for its radium-rich springs and high natural radiation.<ref name="ra">{{Cite journal|title=Exposure to 226Ra from Vegetable Consumption in Ramsar |url=https://doi.org/10.1016/S0265-931X(02)00108-X }} </ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:放射能泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11498934]] Wikidata name: radioactive hot spring [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This is questionable as I think it requires [[WP:MEDRS]] and I am surprised that jawiki has these claims not only not medically sourced but without medically reliable sources[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:10, 14 March 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:Misasa onsen02n4592.jpg|thumb|right|Kawaraburo at {{ill|Misasa Onsen|ja|三朝温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misasa Onsen|qq=}} , known for its radon spring]] '''Radioactive Spring''' (放射能泉, Hōshanōsen) is a classification of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] based on the {{ill|Hot Spring Types|ja|温泉の泉質|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Types|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} labeling system. According to the {{ill|Onsen Act|ja|温泉法|1=Onsen Act|1=Onsen Act|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Act|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Act|qq=}}, it is categorized as a {{ill|medical spring|ja|温泉#療養泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/medical spring|1=onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q655311}} due to the presence of special components.<ref name="JBSJ" /> Approximately 8% of hot springs in Japan fall under this category.<ref name="JBSJ" />
Radioactive spring are often collectively referred to as "radium springs" (ラジウム泉), regardless of their radium content. However, in Japan, most such springs are rich in radon and are commonly called {{Nihongo|Radon Hot Springs|ラドン泉}}. Springs containing high levels of radon's isotope, [[:en:Isotopes_of_radon|Thoron (radon-220]]), are also referred to as {{Nihongo|Thoron Hot Springs|トロン泉}}.<ref>[https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ue3t-cb/bbs/special/sience_of_hotspring/sience_of_hotspring_4-6.htm The Science of Hot Springs: About Radioactive Springs] (関東周辺立ち寄り温泉みしゅらん)</ref> == Definition == {| class="wikitable floatright" style="margin:1em; font-size:95%" |+ Comparison of Old and New Labels<ref>{{Cite web|title=Reference Material: Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=Natural Environment Bureau, Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2018-03-26 }} </ref> ! scope="col" | Old Label ! scope="col" | New Label ! scope="col" | Abbreviated Label |- style="background-color:#eef" | 放射能泉 | {{Plainlist| * Simple Weak Radioactive Spring * Simple Radioactive Spring * Weak Radioactive Spring with Components (e.g., Na, Cl) * Radioactive Spring with Components (e.g., Na, Cl) }}
| {{Plainlist| * Simple Weak Rn Spring * Simple Rn Spring * Weak Rn-Component Spring * Rn-Component Spring }}
|}
; Radioactive Spring : Contains at least 3 nanocuries of radon per kilogram of water (equivalent to 8.25 {{ill|Mache units|en|Mache unit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mache units|qq=}} or 111 {{ill|Becquerel|en|Bq|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Becquerel|qq=}}).<ref name="JBSJ">{{Cite journal|title=Radioactivity and Hot Springs: Onsen Science |author=Masayoshi Yamamoto, Junpei Tomita |journal=Onsen Science |publisher=Japan Society of Hot Spring Science |volume=64 |issue=4 |pages=388-401 |naid=40020464241 |year=2015 }} </ref>
; Weak Radioactive Spring : Contains 8.25–50 Mache units of radon per kilogram. ; Radioactive Spring : Contains 50 Mache units or more of radon per kilogram.
If these criteria are met, the onsen is categorized as a radioactive spring, and many springs are labeled as “含放射能○○泉” or “含弱放射能□□泉” (e.g., “Weak Rn-Na-Cl Spring”). The highest recorded level in Japan is at the Tenmangu Spring in [[:en:Arima Onsen|Arima Onsen]] , with 14 Bq/L.<ref name=JBSJ />
== Usage == {{main|:en:Radiation hormesis#Environmental Use of Radioactive Springs}}
Radioactive spring contain trace amounts of {{ill|radium|en|Radium|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/radium|qq=}} , {{ill|radon|en|Radon|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/radon|qq=}} , {{ill|astatine|en|Astatine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astatine|qq=}} , and other radioactive isotopes from {{ill|nuclear decay|en|Decay (radioactivity)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nuclear decay|qq=}} . For instance, near the source at Arima Onsen, exposure levels reach 13 μSv/h, which is comparable to levels requiring indoor sheltering under Japan's nuclear safety guidelines.
Proponents claim no adverse health effects and argue for {{ill|hormesis|en|Radiation hormesis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hormesis|qq=}} effects, asserting that radiation activates immune cells and promotes health. Critics point out potential health risks from radon exposure. Thus, radioactive springs and the hormesis theory remain subjects of both support and skepticism.
One study estimated a radiation dose of 2.8 nSv from a 2-hour bath in water containing 3.9 Bq/kg of radon. Adding 500 ml of drinking water from the spring increases the dose to 5.1 nSv. At {{ill|Masutomi Onsen|ja|増富温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masutomi Onsen|qq=}} , drinking 2.2 L/day of water with 617 mBq/L radon results in an annual dose of 0.14 mSv.<ref name=JBSJ />
== Health Benefits == ''Note: [[Health effects of hot springs|Effects]] are not guaranteed for all individuals.''
=== Indications === ==== Bathing ==== * {{ill|General Indications for hot spring use|lt=General indications|ja|一般的適応症|1=General Indications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|qq=}}, {{ill|Gout|en|Gout|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gout|qq=}} , {{ill|Hypertension|en|High blood pressure|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hypertension|qq=}} , {{ill|Atherosclerosis|en|Atherosclerosis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atherosclerosis|qq=}} , chronic {{ill|Skin disease|en|Skin disease|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Skin disease|qq=}} , chronic {{ill|Gynecological diseases|en|Gynecology|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gynecological diseases|qq=}} , chronic {{ill|Cholecystitis|en|Cholecystitis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cholecystitis|qq=}} , and {{ill|Cholelithiasis|en|Gallstone|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cholelithiasis|qq=}} . ==== Drinking ==== * Gout, chronic gastrointestinal diseases, chronic cholecystitis, gallstones, {{ill|Neuralgia|en|Neuralgia|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Neuralgia|qq=}}, {{ill|Muscle pain|en|Myalgia|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muscle pain|qq=}} , and joint pain. === Contraindications === * General contraindications for {{ill|Hot springs|ja|温泉#一般的禁忌症|1=General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|lt=General Contraindications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot springs|13=User:Immanuelle/General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|qq=}} apply. == Notable Hot Springs == === In Japan === * Futamata Radium Onsen (Hokkaido) * {{ill|Misasa Onsen|ja|三朝温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misasa Onsen|qq=}}(Tottori)
* {{ill|Sekigane Onsen|ja|関金温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekigane Onsen|qq=}}(Tottori)
* [[:en:Arima Onsen|Arima Onsen]](Hyōgo)
* {{ill|Masutomi Onsen|ja|増富温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masutomi Onsen|qq=}}(Yamanashi)
* [[:en:Tamagawa Hot Spring|Tamagawa Onsen (Akita)]](Akita)
* Ena Gorge Onsen (Gifu) * Ikeda Radium Onsen (Shimane) * {{ill|Bobata Onsen|ja|母畑温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bobata Onsen|qq=}}(Fukushima)
* {{ill|Nekodaki Onsen|ja|猫啼温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekodaki Onsen|qq=}}(Fukushima)

=== Outside Japan === * {{ill|Arkaroola|en|Arkaroola|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arkaroola|qq=}}– Australia
* [[:en:Ramsar,_Iran|Ramsar, Iran]]– Known for its radium-rich springs and high natural radiation.<ref name="ra">{{Cite journal|title=Exposure to 226Ra from Vegetable Consumption in Ramsar |url=https://doi.org/10.1016/S0265-931X(02)00108-X }} </ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:放射能泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11498934]] Wikidata name: radioactive hot spring [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This is questionable as I think it requires [[WP:MEDRS]] and I am surprised that jawiki has these claims not only not medically sourced but without medically reliable sources[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:10, 14 March 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:Misasa onsen02n4592.jpg|thumb|right|Kawaraburo at {{ill|Misasa Onsen|ja|三朝温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misasa Onsen|qq=}} , known for its radon spring]] '''Radioactive Spring''' (放射能泉, Hōshanōsen) is a classification of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] based on the {{ill|Hot Spring Types|ja|温泉の泉質|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Types|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} labeling system. According to the {{ill|Onsen Act|ja|温泉法|1=Onsen Act|1=Onsen Act|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Act|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Act|qq=}}, it is categorized as a {{ill|medical spring|ja|温泉#療養泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/medical spring|1=onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q655311}} due to the presence of special components.<ref name="JBSJ" /> Approximately 8% of hot springs in Japan fall under this category.<ref name="JBSJ" />
Radioactive spring are often collectively referred to as "radium springs" (ラジウム泉), regardless of their radium content. However, in Japan, most such springs are rich in radon and are commonly called {{Nihongo|Radon Hot Springs|ラドン泉|Radon Izumi}}. Springs containing high levels of radon's isotope, [[:en:Isotopes_of_radon|Thoron (radon-220]]), are also referred to as {{Nihongo|Thoron Hot Springs|トロン泉|Toron Izumi}}.<ref>[https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ue3t-cb/bbs/special/sience_of_hotspring/sience_of_hotspring_4-6.htm The Science of Hot Springs: About Radioactive Springs] (関東周辺立ち寄り温泉みしゅらん)</ref> == Definition == {| class="wikitable floatright" style="margin:1em; font-size:95%" |+ Comparison of Old and New Labels<ref>{{Cite web|title=Reference Material: Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=Natural Environment Bureau, Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2018-03-26 }} </ref> ! scope="col" | Old Label ! scope="col" | New Label ! scope="col" | Abbreviated Label |- style="background-color:#eef" | 放射能泉 | {{Plainlist| * Simple Weak Radioactive Spring * Simple Radioactive Spring * Weak Radioactive Spring with Components (e.g., Na, Cl) * Radioactive Spring with Components (e.g., Na, Cl) }}
| {{Plainlist| * Simple Weak Rn Spring * Simple Rn Spring * Weak Rn-Component Spring * Rn-Component Spring }}
|}
; Radioactive Spring : Contains at least 3 nanocuries of radon per kilogram of water (equivalent to 8.25 {{ill|Mache units|en|Mache unit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mache units|qq=}} or 111 {{ill|Becquerel|en|Bq|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Becquerel|qq=}}).<ref name="JBSJ">{{Cite journal|title=Radioactivity and Hot Springs: Onsen Science |author=Masayoshi Yamamoto, Junpei Tomita |journal=Onsen Science |publisher=Japan Society of Hot Spring Science |volume=64 |issue=4 |pages=388-401 |naid=40020464241 |year=2015 }} </ref>
; Weak Radioactive Spring : Contains 8.25–50 Mache units of radon per kilogram. ; Radioactive Spring : Contains 50 Mache units or more of radon per kilogram.
If these criteria are met, the onsen is categorized as a radioactive spring, and many springs are labeled as “含放射能○○泉” or “含弱放射能□□泉” (e.g., “Weak Rn-Na-Cl Spring”). The highest recorded level in Japan is at the Tenmangu Spring in [[:en:Arima Onsen|Arima Onsen]] , with 14 Bq/L.<ref name=JBSJ />
== Usage == {{main|:en:Radiation hormesis#Environmental Use of Radioactive Springs}}
Radioactive spring contain trace amounts of {{ill|radium|en|Radium|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/radium|qq=}} , {{ill|radon|en|Radon|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/radon|qq=}} , {{ill|astatine|en|Astatine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astatine|qq=}} , and other radioactive isotopes from {{ill|nuclear decay|en|Decay (radioactivity)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nuclear decay|qq=}} . For instance, near the source at Arima Onsen, exposure levels reach 13 μSv/h, which is comparable to levels requiring indoor sheltering under Japan's nuclear safety guidelines.
Proponents claim no adverse health effects and argue for {{ill|hormesis|en|Radiation hormesis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hormesis|qq=}} effects, asserting that radiation activates immune cells and promotes health. Critics point out potential health risks from radon exposure. Thus, radioactive springs and the hormesis theory remain subjects of both support and skepticism.
One study estimated a radiation dose of 2.8 nSv from a 2-hour bath in water containing 3.9 Bq/kg of radon. Adding 500 ml of drinking water from the spring increases the dose to 5.1 nSv. At {{ill|Masutomi Onsen|ja|増富温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masutomi Onsen|qq=}} , drinking 2.2 L/day of water with 617 mBq/L radon results in an annual dose of 0.14 mSv.<ref name=JBSJ />
== Health Benefits == ''Note: [[Health effects of hot springs|Effects]] are not guaranteed for all individuals.''
=== Indications === ==== Bathing ==== * {{ill|General Indications for hot spring use|lt=General indications|ja|一般的適応症|1=General Indications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|qq=}}, {{ill|Gout|en|Gout|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gout|qq=}} , {{ill|Hypertension|en|High blood pressure|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hypertension|qq=}} , {{ill|Atherosclerosis|en|Atherosclerosis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atherosclerosis|qq=}} , chronic {{ill|Skin disease|en|Skin disease|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Skin disease|qq=}} , chronic {{ill|Gynecological diseases|en|Gynecology|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gynecological diseases|qq=}} , chronic {{ill|Cholecystitis|en|Cholecystitis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cholecystitis|qq=}} , and {{ill|Cholelithiasis|en|Gallstone|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cholelithiasis|qq=}} . ==== Drinking ==== * Gout, chronic gastrointestinal diseases, chronic cholecystitis, gallstones, {{ill|Neuralgia|en|Neuralgia|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Neuralgia|qq=}}, {{ill|Muscle pain|en|Myalgia|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muscle pain|qq=}} , and joint pain. === Contraindications === * General contraindications for {{ill|Hot springs|ja|温泉#一般的禁忌症|1=General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|lt=General Contraindications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot springs|13=User:Immanuelle/General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|qq=}} apply. == Notable Hot Springs == === In Japan === * Futamata Radium Onsen (Hokkaido) * {{ill|Misasa Onsen|ja|三朝温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misasa Onsen|qq=}}(Tottori)
* {{ill|Sekigane Onsen|ja|関金温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekigane Onsen|qq=}}(Tottori)
* [[:en:Arima Onsen|Arima Onsen]](Hyōgo)
* {{ill|Masutomi Onsen|ja|増富温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masutomi Onsen|qq=}}(Yamanashi)
* [[:en:Tamagawa Hot Spring|Tamagawa Onsen (Akita)]](Akita)
* Ena Gorge Onsen (Gifu) * Ikeda Radium Onsen (Shimane) * {{ill|Bobata Onsen|ja|母畑温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bobata Onsen|qq=}}(Fukushima)
* {{ill|Nekodaki Onsen|ja|猫啼温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekodaki Onsen|qq=}}(Fukushima)
* Baba-no-Yu Onsen (Fukushima)
=== Outside Japan === * {{ill|Arkaroola|en|Arkaroola|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arkaroola|qq=}}– Australia
* [[:en:Ramsar,_Iran|Ramsar, Iran]]– Known for its radium-rich springs and high natural radiation.<ref name="ra">{{Cite journal|title=Exposure to 226Ra from Vegetable Consumption in Ramsar |url=https://doi.org/10.1016/S0265-931X(02)00108-X }} </ref>
* {{ill|Radium Hot Springs|en|Radium Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radium Hot Springs|qq=}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Radiation hormesis|en|Radiation hormesis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radiation hormesis|qq=}}
* {{ill|Natural radiation|en|Natural radiation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natural radiation|qq=}}
* {{ill|Environmental radiation|en|Environmental radiation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Environmental radiation|qq=}}
* {{ill|Natural radioactivity|en|Natural radioactivity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natural radioactivity|qq=}}
* [[:en:Radium_Springs|Radium Springs]]
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Onsen}}

=== Indications === ==== Bathing ==== * {{ill|General Indications for hot spring use|lt=General indications|ja|一般的適応症|1=General Indications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|qq=}}, {{ill|Gout|en|Gout|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gout|qq=}} , {{ill|Hypertension|en|High blood pressure|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hypertension|qq=}} , {{ill|Atherosclerosis|en|Atherosclerosis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atherosclerosis|qq=}} , chronic {{ill|Skin disease|en|Skin disease|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Skin disease|qq=}} , chronic {{ill|Gynecological diseases|en|Gynecology|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gynecological diseases|qq=}} , chronic {{ill|Cholecystitis|en|Cholecystitis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cholecystitis|qq=}} , and {{ill|Cholelithiasis|en|Gallstone|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cholelithiasis|qq=}} . ==== Drinking ==== * Gout, chronic gastrointestinal diseases, chronic cholecystitis, gallstones, {{ill|Neuralgia|en|Neuralgia|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Neuralgia|qq=}}, {{ill|Muscle pain|en|Myalgia|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muscle pain|qq=}} , and joint pain. === Contraindications === * General contraindications for {{ill|Hot springs|ja|温泉#一般的禁忌症|1=General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|lt=General Contraindications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot springs|13=User:Immanuelle/General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|qq=}} apply. == Notable Hot Springs == === In Japan === * Futamata Radium Onsen (Hokkaido) * {{ill|Misasa Onsen|ja|三朝温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misasa Onsen|qq=}}(Tottori)
* {{ill|Sekigane Onsen|ja|関金温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekigane Onsen|qq=}}(Tottori)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:放射能泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11498934]] Wikidata name: radioactive hot spring [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This is questionable as I think it requires [[WP:MEDRS]] and I am surprised that jawiki has these claims not only not medically sourced but without medically reliable sources[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:10, 14 March 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:Misasa onsen02n4592.jpg|thumb|right|Kawaraburo at {{ill|Misasa Onsen|ja|三朝温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misasa Onsen|qq=}} , known for its radon spring]] '''Radioactive Spring''' (放射能泉, Hōshanōsen) is a classification of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] based on the {{ill|Hot Spring Types|ja|温泉の泉質|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Types|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} labeling system. According to the {{ill|Onsen Act|ja|温泉法|1=Onsen Act|1=Onsen Act|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Act|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Act|qq=}}, it is categorized as a {{ill|medical spring|ja|温泉#療養泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/medical spring|1=onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q655311}} due to the presence of special components.<ref name="JBSJ" /> Approximately 8% of hot springs in Japan fall under this category.<ref name="JBSJ" />
Radioactive spring are often collectively referred to as "radium springs" (ラジウム泉), regardless of their radium content. However, in Japan, most such springs are rich in radon and are commonly called {{Nihongo|Radon Hot Springs|ラドン泉|Radon Izumi}}. Springs containing high levels of radon's isotope, [[:en:Isotopes_of_radon|Thoron (radon-220]]), are also referred to as {{Nihongo|Thoron Hot Springs|トロン泉|Toron Izumi}}.<ref>[https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ue3t-cb/bbs/special/sience_of_hotspring/sience_of_hotspring_4-6.htm The Science of Hot Springs: About Radioactive Springs] (関東周辺立ち寄り温泉みしゅらん)</ref> == Definition == {| class="wikitable floatright" style="margin:1em; font-size:95%" |+ Comparison of Old and New Labels<ref>{{Cite web|title=Reference Material: Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=Natural Environment Bureau, Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2018-03-26 }} </ref> ! scope="col" | Old Label ! scope="col" | New Label ! scope="col" | Abbreviated Label |- style="background-color:#eef" | 放射能泉 | {{Plainlist| * Simple Weak Radioactive Spring * Simple Radioactive Spring * Weak Radioactive Spring with Components (e.g., Na, Cl) * Radioactive Spring with Components (e.g., Na, Cl) }}
| {{Plainlist| * Simple Weak Rn Spring * Simple Rn Spring * Weak Rn-Component Spring * Rn-Component Spring }}
|}
; Radioactive Spring : Contains at least 3 nanocuries of radon per kilogram of water (equivalent to 8.25 {{ill|Mache units|en|Mache unit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mache units|qq=}} or 111 {{ill|Becquerel|en|Bq|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Becquerel|qq=}}).<ref name="JBSJ">{{Cite journal|title=Radioactivity and Hot Springs: Onsen Science |author=Masayoshi Yamamoto, Junpei Tomita |journal=Onsen Science |publisher=Japan Society of Hot Spring Science |volume=64 |issue=4 |pages=388-401 |naid=40020464241 |year=2015 }} </ref>
; Weak Radioactive Spring : Contains 8.25–50 Mache units of radon per kilogram. ; Radioactive Spring : Contains 50 Mache units or more of radon per kilogram.
If these criteria are met, the onsen is categorized as a radioactive spring, and many springs are labeled as “含放射能○○泉” or “含弱放射能□□泉” (e.g., “Weak Rn-Na-Cl Spring”). The highest recorded level in Japan is at the Tenmangu Spring in [[:en:Arima Onsen|Arima Onsen]] , with 14 Bq/L.<ref name=JBSJ />
== Usage == {{main|:en:Radiation hormesis#Environmental Use of Radioactive Springs}}
Radioactive spring contain trace amounts of {{ill|radium|en|Radium|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/radium|qq=}} , {{ill|radon|en|Radon|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/radon|qq=}} , {{ill|astatine|en|Astatine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astatine|qq=}} , and other radioactive isotopes from {{ill|nuclear decay|en|Decay (radioactivity)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nuclear decay|qq=}} . For instance, near the source at Arima Onsen, exposure levels reach 13 μSv/h, which is comparable to levels requiring indoor sheltering under Japan's nuclear safety guidelines.
Proponents claim no adverse health effects and argue for {{ill|hormesis|en|Radiation hormesis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hormesis|qq=}} effects, asserting that radiation activates immune cells and promotes health. Critics point out potential health risks from radon exposure. Thus, radioactive springs and the hormesis theory remain subjects of both support and skepticism.
One study estimated a radiation dose of 2.8 nSv from a 2-hour bath in water containing 3.9 Bq/kg of radon. Adding 500 ml of drinking water from the spring increases the dose to 5.1 nSv. At {{ill|Masutomi Onsen|ja|増富温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masutomi Onsen|qq=}} , drinking 2.2 L/day of water with 617 mBq/L radon results in an annual dose of 0.14 mSv.<ref name=JBSJ />
== Health Benefits == ''Note: [[Health effects of hot springs|Effects]] are not guaranteed for all individuals.''

* {{ill|Masutomi Onsen|ja|増富温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masutomi Onsen|qq=}}(Yamanashi)

* {{ill|Nekodaki Onsen|ja|猫啼温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekodaki Onsen|qq=}}(Fukushima)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:放射能泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11498934]] Wikidata name: radioactive hot spring [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This is questionable as I think it requires [[WP:MEDRS]] and I am surprised that jawiki has these claims not only not medically sourced but without medically reliable sources[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:10, 14 March 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:Misasa onsen02n4592.jpg|thumb|right|Kawaraburo at {{ill|Misasa Onsen|ja|三朝温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misasa Onsen|qq=}} , known for its radon spring]] '''Radioactive Spring''' (放射能泉, Hōshanōsen) is a classification of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] based on the {{ill|Hot Spring Types|ja|温泉の泉質|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Types|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} labeling system. According to the {{ill|Onsen Act|ja|温泉法|1=Onsen Act|1=Onsen Act|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Act|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Act|qq=}}, it is categorized as a {{ill|medical spring|ja|温泉#療養泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/medical spring|1=onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q655311}} due to the presence of special components.<ref name="JBSJ" /> Approximately 8% of hot springs in Japan fall under this category.<ref name="JBSJ" />
Radioactive spring are often collectively referred to as "radium springs" (ラジウム泉), regardless of their radium content. However, in Japan, most such springs are rich in radon and are commonly called {{Nihongo|Radon Hot Springs|ラドン泉|Radon Izumi}}. Springs containing high levels of radon's isotope, [[:en:Isotopes_of_radon|Thoron (radon-220]]), are also referred to as {{Nihongo|Thoron Hot Springs|トロン泉|Toron Izumi}}.<ref>[https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ue3t-cb/bbs/special/sience_of_hotspring/sience_of_hotspring_4-6.htm The Science of Hot Springs: About Radioactive Springs] (関東周辺立ち寄り温泉みしゅらん)</ref> == Definition == {| class="wikitable floatright" style="margin:1em; font-size:95%" |+ Comparison of Old and New Labels<ref>{{Cite web|title=Reference Material: Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=Natural Environment Bureau, Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2018-03-26 }} </ref> ! scope="col" | Old Label ! scope="col" | New Label ! scope="col" | Abbreviated Label |- style="background-color:#eef" | 放射能泉 | {{Plainlist| * Simple Weak Radioactive Spring * Simple Radioactive Spring * Weak Radioactive Spring with Components (e.g., Na, Cl) * Radioactive Spring with Components (e.g., Na, Cl) }}
| {{Plainlist| * Simple Weak Rn Spring * Simple Rn Spring * Weak Rn-Component Spring * Rn-Component Spring }}
|}
; Radioactive Spring : Contains at least 3 nanocuries of radon per kilogram of water (equivalent to 8.25 {{ill|Mache units|en|Mache unit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mache units|qq=}} or 111 {{ill|Becquerel|en|Bq|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Becquerel|qq=}}).<ref name="JBSJ">{{Cite journal|title=Radioactivity and Hot Springs: Onsen Science |author=Masayoshi Yamamoto, Junpei Tomita |journal=Onsen Science |publisher=Japan Society of Hot Spring Science |volume=64 |issue=4 |pages=388-401 |naid=40020464241 |year=2015 }} </ref>
; Weak Radioactive Spring : Contains 8.25–50 Mache units of radon per kilogram. ; Radioactive Spring : Contains 50 Mache units or more of radon per kilogram.
If these criteria are met, the onsen is categorized as a radioactive spring, and many springs are labeled as “含放射能○○泉” or “含弱放射能□□泉” (e.g., “Weak Rn-Na-Cl Spring”). The highest recorded level in Japan is at the Tenmangu Spring in [[:en:Arima Onsen|Arima Onsen]] , with 14 Bq/L.<ref name=JBSJ />
== Usage == {{main|:en:Radiation hormesis#Environmental Use of Radioactive Springs}}
Radioactive spring contain trace amounts of {{ill|radium|en|Radium|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/radium|qq=}} , {{ill|radon|en|Radon|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/radon|qq=}} , {{ill|astatine|en|Astatine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astatine|qq=}} , and other radioactive isotopes from {{ill|nuclear decay|en|Decay (radioactivity)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nuclear decay|qq=}} . For instance, near the source at Arima Onsen, exposure levels reach 13 μSv/h, which is comparable to levels requiring indoor sheltering under Japan's nuclear safety guidelines.
Proponents claim no adverse health effects and argue for {{ill|hormesis|en|Radiation hormesis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hormesis|qq=}} effects, asserting that radiation activates immune cells and promotes health. Critics point out potential health risks from radon exposure. Thus, radioactive springs and the hormesis theory remain subjects of both support and skepticism.
One study estimated a radiation dose of 2.8 nSv from a 2-hour bath in water containing 3.9 Bq/kg of radon. Adding 500 ml of drinking water from the spring increases the dose to 5.1 nSv. At {{ill|Masutomi Onsen|ja|増富温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masutomi Onsen|qq=}} , drinking 2.2 L/day of water with 617 mBq/L radon results in an annual dose of 0.14 mSv.<ref name=JBSJ />
== Health Benefits == ''Note: [[Health effects of hot springs|Effects]] are not guaranteed for all individuals.''
=== Indications === ==== Bathing ==== * {{ill|General Indications for hot spring use|lt=General indications|ja|一般的適応症|1=General Indications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|qq=}}, {{ill|Gout|en|Gout|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gout|qq=}} , {{ill|Hypertension|en|High blood pressure|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hypertension|qq=}} , {{ill|Atherosclerosis|en|Atherosclerosis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atherosclerosis|qq=}} , chronic {{ill|Skin disease|en|Skin disease|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Skin disease|qq=}} , chronic {{ill|Gynecological diseases|en|Gynecology|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gynecological diseases|qq=}} , chronic {{ill|Cholecystitis|en|Cholecystitis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cholecystitis|qq=}} , and {{ill|Cholelithiasis|en|Gallstone|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cholelithiasis|qq=}} . ==== Drinking ==== * Gout, chronic gastrointestinal diseases, chronic cholecystitis, gallstones, {{ill|Neuralgia|en|Neuralgia|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Neuralgia|qq=}}, {{ill|Muscle pain|en|Myalgia|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muscle pain|qq=}} , and joint pain. === Contraindications === * General contraindications for {{ill|Hot springs|ja|温泉#一般的禁忌症|1=General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|lt=General Contraindications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot springs|13=User:Immanuelle/General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|qq=}} apply. == Notable Hot Springs == === In Japan === * Futamata Radium Onsen (Hokkaido) * {{ill|Misasa Onsen|ja|三朝温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misasa Onsen|qq=}}(Tottori)
* {{ill|Sekigane Onsen|ja|関金温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekigane Onsen|qq=}}(Tottori)
* [[:en:Arima Onsen|Arima Onsen]](Hyōgo)
* {{ill|Masutomi Onsen|ja|増富温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masutomi Onsen|qq=}}(Yamanashi)
* [[:en:Tamagawa Hot Spring|Tamagawa Onsen (Akita)]](Akita)
* Ena Gorge Onsen (Gifu) * Ikeda Radium Onsen (Shimane) * {{ill|Bobata Onsen|ja|母畑温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bobata Onsen|qq=}}(Fukushima)
* {{ill|Nekodaki Onsen|ja|猫啼温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekodaki Onsen|qq=}}(Fukushima)
* Baba-no-Yu Onsen (Fukushima)
=== Outside Japan === * {{ill|Arkaroola|en|Arkaroola|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arkaroola|qq=}}– Australia
* [[:en:Ramsar,_Iran|Ramsar, Iran]]– Known for its radium-rich springs and high natural radiation.<ref name="ra">{{Cite journal|title=Exposure to 226Ra from Vegetable Consumption in Ramsar |url=https://doi.org/10.1016/S0265-931X(02)00108-X }} </ref>
* {{ill|Radium Hot Springs|en|Radium Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radium Hot Springs|qq=}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Radiation hormesis|en|Radiation hormesis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radiation hormesis|qq=}}
* {{ill|Natural radiation|en|Natural radiation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natural radiation|qq=}}
* {{ill|Environmental radiation|en|Environmental radiation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Environmental radiation|qq=}}
* {{ill|Natural radioactivity|en|Natural radioactivity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natural radioactivity|qq=}}
* [[:en:Radium_Springs|Radium Springs]]

=== Indications === ==== Bathing ==== * {{ill|General Indications for hot spring use|lt=General indications|ja|一般的適応症|1=General Indications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|qq=}}, {{ill|Gout|en|Gout|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gout|qq=}} , {{ill|Hypertension|en|High blood pressure|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hypertension|qq=}} , {{ill|Atherosclerosis|en|Atherosclerosis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atherosclerosis|qq=}} , chronic {{ill|Skin disease|en|Skin disease|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Skin disease|qq=}} , chronic {{ill|Gynecological diseases|en|Gynecology|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gynecological diseases|qq=}} , chronic {{ill|Cholecystitis|en|Cholecystitis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cholecystitis|qq=}} , and {{ill|Cholelithiasis|en|Gallstone|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cholelithiasis|qq=}} . ==== Drinking ==== * Gout, chronic gastrointestinal diseases, chronic cholecystitis, gallstones, {{ill|Neuralgia|en|Neuralgia|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Neuralgia|qq=}}, {{ill|Muscle pain|en|Myalgia|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muscle pain|qq=}} , and joint pain. === Contraindications === * General contraindications for {{ill|Hot springs|ja|温泉#一般的禁忌症|1=General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|lt=General Contraindications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot springs|13=User:Immanuelle/General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|qq=}} apply. == Notable Hot Springs == === In Japan === * Futamata Radium Onsen (Hokkaido) * {{ill|Misasa Onsen|ja|三朝温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misasa Onsen|qq=}}(Tottori)
* {{ill|Sekigane Onsen|ja|関金温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekigane Onsen|qq=}}(Tottori)
* [[:en:Arima Onsen|Arima Onsen]](Hyōgo)
* {{ill|Masutomi Onsen|ja|増富温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masutomi Onsen|qq=}}(Yamanashi)
* [[:en:Tamagawa Hot Spring|Tamagawa Onsen (Akita)]](Akita)
* Ena Gorge Onsen (Gifu) * Ikeda Radium Onsen (Shimane) * {{ill|Bobata Onsen|ja|母畑温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bobata Onsen|qq=}}(Fukushima)
* {{ill|Nekodaki Onsen|ja|猫啼温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekodaki Onsen|qq=}}(Fukushima)
* Baba-no-Yu Onsen (Fukushima)
=== Outside Japan === * {{ill|Arkaroola|en|Arkaroola|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arkaroola|qq=}}– Australia
* [[:en:Ramsar,_Iran|Ramsar, Iran]]– Known for its radium-rich springs and high natural radiation.<ref name="ra">{{Cite journal|title=Exposure to 226Ra from Vegetable Consumption in Ramsar |url=https://doi.org/10.1016/S0265-931X(02)00108-X }} </ref>
* {{ill|Radium Hot Springs|en|Radium Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radium Hot Springs|qq=}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Radiation hormesis|en|Radiation hormesis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radiation hormesis|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:放射能泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:放射能泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:放射能泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11498934]] Wikidata name: radioactive hot spring [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This is questionable as I think it requires [[WP:MEDRS]] and I am surprised that jawiki has these claims not only not medically sourced but without medically reliable sources[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:10, 14 March 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:Misasa onsen02n4592.jpg|thumb|right|Kawaraburo at {{ill|Misasa Onsen|ja|三朝温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misasa Onsen|qq=}} , known for its radon spring]] '''Radioactive Spring''' (放射能泉, Hōshanōsen) is a classification of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] based on the {{ill|Hot Spring Types|ja|温泉の泉質|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Types|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} labeling system. According to the {{ill|Onsen Act|ja|温泉法|1=Onsen Act|1=Onsen Act|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Act|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Act|qq=}}, it is categorized as a {{ill|medical spring|ja|温泉#療養泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/medical spring|1=onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q655311}} due to the presence of special components.<ref name="JBSJ" /> Approximately 8% of hot springs in Japan fall under this category.<ref name="JBSJ" />
Radioactive spring are often collectively referred to as "radium springs" (ラジウム泉), regardless of their radium content. However, in Japan, most such springs are rich in radon and are commonly called {{Nihongo|Radon Hot Springs|ラドン泉|Radon Izumi}}. Springs containing high levels of radon's isotope, [[:en:Isotopes_of_radon|Thoron (radon-220]]), are also referred to as {{Nihongo|Thoron Hot Springs|トロン泉|Toron Izumi}}.<ref>[https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ue3t-cb/bbs/special/sience_of_hotspring/sience_of_hotspring_4-6.htm The Science of Hot Springs: About Radioactive Springs] (関東周辺立ち寄り温泉みしゅらん)</ref> == Definition == {| class="wikitable floatright" style="margin:1em; font-size:95%" |+ Comparison of Old and New Labels<ref>{{Cite web|title=Reference Material: Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=Natural Environment Bureau, Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2018-03-26 }} </ref> ! scope="col" | Old Label ! scope="col" | New Label ! scope="col" | Abbreviated Label |- style="background-color:#eef" | 放射能泉 | {{Plainlist| * Simple Weak Radioactive Spring * Simple Radioactive Spring * Weak Radioactive Spring with Components (e.g., Na, Cl) * Radioactive Spring with Components (e.g., Na, Cl) }}
| {{Plainlist| * Simple Weak Rn Spring * Simple Rn Spring * Weak Rn-Component Spring * Rn-Component Spring }}
|}
; Radioactive Spring : Contains at least 3 nanocuries of radon per kilogram of water (equivalent to 8.25 {{ill|Mache units|en|Mache unit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mache units|qq=}} or 111 {{ill|Becquerel|en|Bq|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Becquerel|qq=}}).<ref name="JBSJ">{{Cite journal|title=Radioactivity and Hot Springs: Onsen Science |author=Masayoshi Yamamoto, Junpei Tomita |journal=Onsen Science |publisher=Japan Society of Hot Spring Science |volume=64 |issue=4 |pages=388-401 |naid=40020464241 |year=2015 }} </ref>
; Weak Radioactive Spring : Contains 8.25–50 Mache units of radon per kilogram. ; Radioactive Spring : Contains 50 Mache units or more of radon per kilogram.
If these criteria are met, the onsen is categorized as a radioactive spring, and many springs are labeled as “含放射能○○泉” or “含弱放射能□□泉” (e.g., “Weak Rn-Na-Cl Spring”). The highest recorded level in Japan is at the Tenmangu Spring in [[:en:Arima Onsen|Arima Onsen]] , with 14 Bq/L.<ref name=JBSJ />
== Usage == {{main|:en:Radiation hormesis#Environmental Use of Radioactive Springs}}
Radioactive spring contain trace amounts of {{ill|radium|en|Radium|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/radium|qq=}} , {{ill|radon|en|Radon|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/radon|qq=}} , {{ill|astatine|en|Astatine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astatine|qq=}} , and other radioactive isotopes from {{ill|nuclear decay|en|Decay (radioactivity)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nuclear decay|qq=}} . For instance, near the source at Arima Onsen, exposure levels reach 13 μSv/h, which is comparable to levels requiring indoor sheltering under Japan's nuclear safety guidelines.
Proponents claim no adverse health effects and argue for {{ill|hormesis|en|Radiation hormesis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hormesis|qq=}} effects, asserting that radiation activates immune cells and promotes health. Critics point out potential health risks from radon exposure. Thus, radioactive springs and the hormesis theory remain subjects of both support and skepticism.
One study estimated a radiation dose of 2.8 nSv from a 2-hour bath in water containing 3.9 Bq/kg of radon. Adding 500 ml of drinking water from the spring increases the dose to 5.1 nSv. At {{ill|Masutomi Onsen|ja|増富温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masutomi Onsen|qq=}} , drinking 2.2 L/day of water with 617 mBq/L radon results in an annual dose of 0.14 mSv.<ref name=JBSJ />
== Health Benefits == ''Note: [[Health effects of hot springs|Effects]] are not guaranteed for all individuals.''
=== Indications === ==== Bathing ==== * {{ill|General Indications for hot spring use|lt=General indications|ja|一般的適応症|1=General Indications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|qq=}}, {{ill|Gout|en|Gout|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gout|qq=}} , {{ill|Hypertension|en|High blood pressure|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hypertension|qq=}} , {{ill|Atherosclerosis|en|Atherosclerosis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atherosclerosis|qq=}} , chronic {{ill|Skin disease|en|Skin disease|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Skin disease|qq=}} , chronic {{ill|Gynecological diseases|en|Gynecology|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gynecological diseases|qq=}} , chronic {{ill|Cholecystitis|en|Cholecystitis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cholecystitis|qq=}} , and {{ill|Cholelithiasis|en|Gallstone|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cholelithiasis|qq=}} . ==== Drinking ==== * Gout, chronic gastrointestinal diseases, chronic cholecystitis, gallstones, {{ill|Neuralgia|en|Neuralgia|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Neuralgia|qq=}}, {{ill|Muscle pain|en|Myalgia|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muscle pain|qq=}} , and joint pain. === Contraindications === * General contraindications for {{ill|Hot springs|ja|温泉#一般的禁忌症|1=General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|lt=General Contraindications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot springs|13=User:Immanuelle/General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|qq=}} apply. == Notable Hot Springs == === In Japan === * Futamata Radium Onsen (Hokkaido) * {{ill|Misasa Onsen|ja|三朝温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misasa Onsen|qq=}}(Tottori)
* {{ill|Sekigane Onsen|ja|関金温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekigane Onsen|qq=}}(Tottori)
* [[:en:Arima Onsen|Arima Onsen]](Hyōgo)
* {{ill|Masutomi Onsen|ja|増富温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masutomi Onsen|qq=}}(Yamanashi)
* [[:en:Tamagawa Hot Spring|Tamagawa Onsen (Akita)]](Akita)
* Ena Gorge Onsen (Gifu) * Ikeda Radium Onsen (Shimane) * {{ill|Bobata Onsen|ja|母畑温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bobata Onsen|qq=}}(Fukushima)
* {{ill|Nekodaki Onsen|ja|猫啼温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekodaki Onsen|qq=}}(Fukushima)
* Baba-no-Yu Onsen (Fukushima)
=== Outside Japan === * {{ill|Arkaroola|en|Arkaroola|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arkaroola|qq=}}– Australia
* [[:en:Ramsar,_Iran|Ramsar, Iran]]– Known for its radium-rich springs and high natural radiation.<ref name="ra">{{Cite journal|title=Exposure to 226Ra from Vegetable Consumption in Ramsar |url=https://doi.org/10.1016/S0265-931X(02)00108-X }} </ref>
* {{ill|Radium Hot Springs|en|Radium Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radium Hot Springs|qq=}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Radiation hormesis|en|Radiation hormesis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radiation hormesis|qq=}}
